reviews.review_msg: Tough teacher, hard to do well if she dislikes you
Avoid, she insults her students and thinks she is better than them. Assumes they dont know anything

Taking the notoriously difficult MBB 331 course with Dr. K was challenging yet rewarding. Graded quizzes are given on paper after each lecture. You may hate this idea but will feel rewarded if you take it seriously and ask questions. The exams were very frustrating and roughly graded, but luckily, they were not the only place to get marks.

Never felt more discouraged in my life. In-class quizzes after every lecture for attendance marks based on the material she teaches that day and mandatory attendance in tutorials. Unlimited cheat sheets for all exams but it won't help you. Exams are all concepts that are not taught. Future students, STUDY THE PRACTICE EXAMS, NOT THE LECTURE SLIDES.

Beware of the critical commentary analysis assignment. Kyle was our TA and he graded probably the hardest I have ever seen in 4 years at SFU taking science courses. My partner and I made sure to get his guidance on what to write. He literally graded as if he did not understand the whole point of what he told us to write. No regrading/explaining.

I can see why she has mostly positive reviews but honestly I think people tend to view through rose coloured glasses due to how tough the other prof for this course is. Her lectures were totally fine and she seemed to care during them however, she NEVER responding to any of our emails and the final was absolutely out of left field.

Really great professor and her exams were fair. The quizzes and problem sets were helpful if you understood them. She is really understanding and helped me arrange my final exam date. The TA was great too and asking questions during tutorial was helping with clarifying things. Overall she made the course really enjoyable.

She was a better prof for 331 considering 331 is really hard in general. She had decent explanations and was very good at lecturing relative to other profs. However she wasnt approachable. She would barely reply to emails or questions and seemed bothered by them, which kinda made her difficult to approach despite her kind tone.

Fair exams and not much experimental content. Some of her resources like her practice midterms (2/3/final) were unhelpful but she reuses her Kahoot and quiz mcqs in exams sometimes. Explanations and approach to teaching concepts can be unclear at times. MCQs in exams were more complex than what she covered in class, especially midterm 3.

When teaching, she would go off topic so often it was hard to understand the challenging concepts and it was often simplified - not reflecting the difficulty of the exams. She's a great person and open for feedback, but lowk catfished by her ratemyprof score. It would help if she updated her slides so it's less confusing, but fair exams + grading.

2nd time taking a class with Prof Sneddon. What a great prof! She is so real, caring, and quite funny, too. Her exams are more than fair, assignments are fair, problem sets are helpful and other resources are just to help you in the course. An amazing prof, if she's teaching a course def take it! 10/10 would recommend
Took in the summer, went well for the effort given. She is really a great prof. Could've gotten better but hey that's summer semester.

Dr. Sneddon is an amazing professor. She is super helpful outside of class in office hours or after lecture. Her exams are fair if you study and attend office hours. She does give hints on what could be on exams and makes learning fun and easy to remember. She makes such a hard course easy to learn! Go to lecture, do the problem sets.

Dr. Sneddon is an amazin prof for MBB331. The grading if very clear and fair. The book is there but you don't actually need to read it as all of the content comes from lectures. Be sure to actually watch the lectures because Dr. Sneddon highlights the important parts. Also, attendance was needed only for tutorials. Lecures were recorded.
Best prof at SFU. 331 is a horrible course to do but this prof makes it enjoyable. super engaging !!!!

Dr. Sneddon made MBB331 a very tolerable course. Dr. Sneddon attempted to make the course as palatable as possible given the otherwise DRY course content. Throughout the semester I found Dr. Sneddon to be incredibly approachable both during office hours and by email. Overall Dr. Sneddon wants her students to do well as evident by her emailresponses

Dr. Sneddon is a wonderful, approachable prof. All of her lectures are recorded but she takes questions during breaks and right after class in-person. She offers extra Kahoots quizzes for most lectures. MBB331 is a hard course bc of dense lectures/short answer qs on exams;Ask qs on each assay!Focus on learning objectives in lectures&problem sets.

Dr Sneddon is an amazing professor. Her lectures are useful. She explains all the concepts clearly. I recommend taking 331 with her. This is a heavy course but Dr Sneddon makes it super interesting. She has clear grading criteria. If you put in effort, you will score well on her exams.

Peter is funny, I didn't like the class material but he makes it bearable. Loves the chalkboard and is nice to chit chat with during office hours. Could be more specific on what will be on the exams, but our TA covered that so. Get a good TA.
Changed my life.

Dr. harden is a wonderful teacher! his exams were very refreshing compared to most other MBB courses in the way that its not just straight memorization, you actually have to think. The final is doable, i spent maybe 3 days studying for it and did ok. He does speak very quietly and can mumble so sit near the front if you want to catch everything!

Go to class!! There is a lot of material and frankly, not much of it is very important.  Study hard for the midterms because the final is killer.  He does speak quietly and tends to mumble sometimes so sit near the front if you can.

Always sit at the front with NO ONE in your vicinity otherwise you will get lost- Talks incredibly quietly. But he's VERY clear and loved how he stated one point in 10 different ways and used analogies often. 1st midterm was hard since it was all about experimental techniques. 2nd was easy. Final was okay, lots of comparisons. Overall, I liked him.

He tends to mumble in his lectures and doesnt use a mic. But lectures are very important. The final was quite hard, a lot of emphasis on comparison between pro and eukaryotes, and experimental techniques. He likes to put in random specific proteins as well, so watch out for those. He barely uses actual material from the notes.

Christopher's lectures were not only long, but his voice droned on for 3 hours (an hour longer than our designated lecture time). His exams were very specific on certain things discussed in lecture yet not covered in lecture slides. Be ready to take lots of notes and read everything he assigns.
Dr. Beh is extremely condescending to his students.

Lectures were very interesting, and he generally seemed like a cool guy. Midterms were fair, he recyled a few questions from problem sets from previous weeks. Make sure you know your restriction maps and primer design questions for MT 1 and Final! Only thing that really bothered me is that answers were not posted for PS's before quizzes/midterms.
Very hard course. Prof is not at all helpful. Gets mad at students for not doing well
Hardcore
